content.body: All living organisms share seven characteristics, remembered as MRS GREN: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, Nutrition.

content.title: MRS GREN Explained
content.body:
Movement: organisms can move (whole body or parts)
Respiration: release energy from food (aerobic/anaerobic)
Sensitivity: detect and respond to stimuli
Growth: increase in size/cell number
Reproduction: produce offspring
Excretion: remove metabolic waste products
Nutrition: obtain and use food for energy

content.title: Living vs Non-Living
content.body:
Fire: moves, respires, grows but does NOT reproduce or excrete waste → not alive
Viruses: reproduce but lack most other characteristics → debated
A car: moves but not alive — all 7 must apply

questions.question: MRS GREN stands for
options: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, Nutrition
questions.answer: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, Nutrition
questions.explanation: MRS GREN is the mnemonic for the seven characteristics of living organisms

questions.question: Excretion in living organisms means
questions.answer: removing metabolic WASTE products
questions.explanation: Excretion removes metabolic waste (CO2, urea, etc.) — not the same as egestion of undigested food

questions.question: A seed is considered alive because
options: it can grow and eventually reproduce
questions.answer: it can grow and eventually reproduce
questions.explanation: Dormant seeds still have potential to grow and reproduce — they are alive

questions.question: Which is NOT a characteristic of living organisms
questions.answer: magnetism
questions.explanation: Magnetism is a physical property, not a biological characteristic of life

questions.question: Sensitivity means
options: detecting and responding to changes in environment (stimuli)
questions.answer: detecting and responding to changes in environment (stimuli)
questions.explanation: Sensitivity: organisms detect stimuli (light, heat, touch) and respond appropriately

questions.question: Respiration in biology means
options: chemical release of energy from food
questions.answer: chemical release of energy from food
questions.explanation: Biological respiration: cellular process releasing energy from glucose (C6H12O6 + 6O2 → 6CO2 + 6H2O + ATP)

questions.question: A flame moves and grows. Why is it NOT alive?
options: it cannot reproduce or excrete waste
questions.answer: it cannot reproduce or excrete waste
questions.explanation: To be alive, all 7 MRS GREN characteristics must apply. A flame lacks reproduction and excretion

questions.question: Growth in living organisms involves
options: an increase in cell number and/or cell size
questions.answer: an increase in cell number and/or cell size
questions.explanation: Biological growth: cell division (mitosis) producing more cells and cell enlargement

questions.question: Nutrition in animals vs plants: animals
options: obtain food by eating other organisms (heterotrophic)
questions.answer: obtain food by eating other organisms (heterotrophic)
questions.explanation: Animals are heterotrophs — they consume food. Plants are autotrophs — they make their own food

questions.question: Reproduction is essential for species because
options: it ensures survival of the species over time
questions.answer: it ensures survival of the species over time
questions.explanation: Without reproduction, a species would go extinct. Individual organisms are mortal but species persist through reproduction

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: The cell
questions.explanation: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for energy production in cells?
questions.answer: Mitochondria
questions.explanation: Mitochondria carry out aerobic respiration to produce ATP — the cell's energy currency. Hence called the 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.question: What is photosynthesis?
options: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
The breakdown of glucose for energy
The absorption of minerals from soil
questions.answer: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light energy → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants convert light energy to chemical energy stored in glucose.

questions.question: What is osmosis?
options: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.answer: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the passive movement of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane from a region of higher water potential to lower.

questions.question: What molecule carries genetic information?
questions.answer: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)
questions.explanation: DNA carries the genetic code in sequences of bases (A, T, G, C) that determine the structure of proteins.

questions.question: What is the function of chloroplasts?
questions.answer: Site of photosynthesis
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis, capturing light energy to make glucose.

questions.question: In genetics, what is a dominant allele?
options: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
One that is only expressed when two copies are present
questions.answer: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
questions.explanation: A dominant allele is expressed in the phenotype regardless of whether it is heterozygous or homozygous.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
options: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.answer: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the passive movement of particles down a concentration gradient — no energy (ATP) is required.

questions.question: Which blood component carries oxygen?
questions.answer: Red blood cells (haemoglobin)
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain haemoglobin, which binds to oxygen in the lungs and releases it in body tissues.

questions.question: What is the role of the nucleus?
options: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.answer: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the control centre of the cell, containing DNA and directing all cellular activities including protein synthesis.

content.body: Scientists classify organisms into groups based on shared characteristics. The binomial system gives each species a two-part Latin name (genus + species).

Classification hierarchy (Kingdom to Species):
Kingdom → Phylum → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species
Mnemonic: King Philip Came Over For Good Soup

Five kingdoms: Monera (bacteria), Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia
Modern: 3 domains: Bacteria, Archaea, Eukarya

content.title: Binomial Nomenclature
content.body:
Homo sapiens (humans)
Genus: Homo (capital first letter)
Species: sapiens (lowercase)
Always italicised or underlined

Panthera leo (lion), Panthera tigris (tiger)
Same genus = closely related

content.title: Major Kingdoms
content.body:
Monera: single-celled, no nucleus (prokaryote). E.g. bacteria
Protista: single-celled with nucleus. E.g. Amoeba, Paramecium
Fungi: absorb nutrients, cell walls of chitin. E.g. mushrooms, yeast
Plantae: photosynthetic, cell walls of cellulose. E.g. ferns, trees
Animalia: multicellular, no cell walls, heterotrophic

questions.question: Correct order of classification hierarchy
questions.answer: Kingdom-Phylum-Class-Order-Family-Genus-Species
questions.explanation: King Philip Came Over For Good Soup

questions.question: In Homo sapiens, 'Homo' is the
questions.answer: genus
questions.explanation: Binomial name = Genus species. Homo is the genus, sapiens is the species

questions.question: Binomial names are always written
questions.answer: in Latin, italicised
questions.explanation: Binomial nomenclature uses Latin/Greek, italicised (or underlined when handwritten)

questions.question: Bacteria belong to kingdom
questions.answer: Monera
questions.explanation: Bacteria are prokaryotes (no nucleus), placed in Monera (or domain Bacteria in modern classification)

questions.question: Fungi differ from plants because fungi
options: lack chlorophyll and absorb nutrients rather than photosynthesize
questions.answer: lack chlorophyll and absorb nutrients rather than photosynthesize
questions.explanation: Fungi are heterotrophic decomposers; they secrete enzymes and absorb digested nutrients

questions.question: Organisms in the same genus are
questions.answer: closely related but distinct species
questions.explanation: Same genus = closely related, share many characteristics but are different species

questions.question: A dichotomous key works by
options: presenting pairs of contrasting characteristics to identify organisms
questions.answer: presenting pairs of contrasting characteristics to identify organisms
questions.explanation: Dichotomous key: at each step, choose one of two options until organism is identified

questions.question: The most specific (smallest) classification group is
questions.answer: Species
questions.explanation: Species is the most specific level; members can interbreed to produce fertile offspring

questions.question: Amoeba belongs to kingdom
questions.answer: Protista
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a single-celled eukaryote (has nucleus) — classified in Protista

questions.question: Panthera leo and Panthera tigris are in the same
questions.answer: genus
questions.explanation: Both share genus Panthera but are different species (leo=lion, tigris=tiger)

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: The cell
questions.explanation: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for energy production in cells?
questions.answer: Mitochondria
questions.explanation: Mitochondria carry out aerobic respiration to produce ATP — the cell's energy currency. Hence called the 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.question: What is photosynthesis?
options: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
The breakdown of glucose for energy
The absorption of minerals from soil
questions.answer: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light energy → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants convert light energy to chemical energy stored in glucose.

questions.question: What is osmosis?
options: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.answer: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the passive movement of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane from a region of higher water potential to lower.

questions.question: What molecule carries genetic information?
questions.answer: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)
questions.explanation: DNA carries the genetic code in sequences of bases (A, T, G, C) that determine the structure of proteins.

questions.question: What is the function of chloroplasts?
questions.answer: Site of photosynthesis
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis, capturing light energy to make glucose.

questions.question: In genetics, what is a dominant allele?
options: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
One that is only expressed when two copies are present
questions.answer: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
questions.explanation: A dominant allele is expressed in the phenotype regardless of whether it is heterozygous or homozygous.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
options: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.answer: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the passive movement of particles down a concentration gradient — no energy (ATP) is required.

questions.question: Which blood component carries oxygen?
questions.answer: Red blood cells (haemoglobin)
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain haemoglobin, which binds to oxygen in the lungs and releases it in body tissues.

questions.question: What is the role of the nucleus?
options: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.answer: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the control centre of the cell, containing DNA and directing all cellular activities including protein synthesis.

content.body:
Vertebrates: have backbone (vertebral column)
5 classes: Fish, Amphibians, Reptiles, Birds, Mammals

Invertebrates: no backbone (~95% of all animals)
Groups: Insects, Arachnids, Crustaceans, Myriapods, Molluscs, Annelids, Echinoderms, Cnidarians, Platyhelminthes (flatworms), Nematodes, Porifera (sponges)

content.title: Vertebrate Classes
content.body:
Fish: gills, scales, cold-blooded, lay eggs in water
Amphibians: moist skin, cold-blooded, eggs in water, breathe through skin+lungs
Reptiles: dry scales, cold-blooded, lay eggs on land (amniotic)
Birds: feathers, warm-blooded, lay hard-shelled eggs, wings
Mammals: hair/fur, warm-blooded, give birth to live young, suckle with milk

content.title: Invertebrate Examples
content.body:
Insects (6 legs): bee, fly, butterfly
Arachnids (8 legs): spider, scorpion, tick
Crustaceans: crab, shrimp, woodlouse
Molluscs: snail, octopus, clam
Annelids: earthworm, leech
Echinoderms: starfish, sea urchin

questions.question: Vertebrates are animals with
questions.answer: a backbone (vertebral column)
questions.explanation: Vertebrates: phylum Chordata with a vertebral column (backbone)

questions.question: A frog is classified as an amphibian because
options: it lives in water and on land, breathes through moist skin and lungs
questions.answer: it lives in water and on land, breathes through moist skin and lungs
questions.explanation: Amphibians live double life (amphi=both): aquatic larvae, terrestrial adults

questions.question: Birds and mammals are both warm-blooded (endothermic), meaning
options: they maintain constant body temperature internally
questions.answer: they maintain constant body temperature internally
questions.explanation: Endotherms generate heat metabolically to maintain constant body temperature

questions.question: Insects have how many legs?
questions.explanation: Insects: class Insecta, 6 legs, 3 body segments (head, thorax, abdomen)

questions.question: Arachnids have
questions.answer: 8 legs
questions.explanation: Spiders, scorpions, mites, ticks — all arachnids with 8 legs

questions.question: What makes mammals unique?
options: fur/hair, warm-blooded, suckle young with milk
questions.answer: fur/hair, warm-blooded, suckle young with milk
questions.explanation: Mammary glands (milk production) is the defining characteristic of mammals

questions.question: Fish breathe using
questions.answer: gills
questions.explanation: Fish extract dissolved oxygen from water using gills

questions.question: Reptiles lay amniotic eggs on land. Amniotic means
options: the egg has a membrane protecting embryo from drying out
questions.answer: the egg has a membrane protecting embryo from drying out
questions.explanation: Amniotic egg has membranes (amnion, allantois) keeping embryo moist on land — key reptile adaptation

questions.question: Earthworms belong to which invertebrate group?
questions.answer: Annelids
questions.explanation: Annelida: segmented worms. Earthworm (Lumbricus terrestris) is a classic annelid

questions.question: The largest group of animals (most species) is
questions.answer: Insects
questions.explanation: Insects are the most speciose animal group — over 1 million described species, ~75% of all animal species

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: The cell
questions.explanation: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for energy production in cells?
questions.answer: Mitochondria
questions.explanation: Mitochondria carry out aerobic respiration to produce ATP — the cell's energy currency. Hence called the 'powerhouse of the cell'.

options: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
The breakdown of glucose for energy
The absorption of minerals from soil
questions.answer: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light energy → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants convert light energy to chemical energy stored in glucose.

options: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.answer: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the passive movement of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane from a region of higher water potential to lower.

questions.answer: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)
questions.explanation: DNA carries the genetic code in sequences of bases (A, T, G, C) that determine the structure of proteins.

questions.question: What is the function of chloroplasts?
questions.answer: Site of photosynthesis
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis, capturing light energy to make glucose.

questions.question: In genetics, what is a dominant allele?
options: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
One that is only expressed when two copies are present
questions.answer: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
questions.explanation: A dominant allele is expressed in the phenotype regardless of whether it is heterozygous or homozygous.

options: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.answer: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the passive movement of particles down a concentration gradient — no energy (ATP) is required.

questions.answer: Red blood cells (haemoglobin)
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain haemoglobin, which binds to oxygen in the lungs and releases it in body tissues.

options: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.answer: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the control centre of the cell, containing DNA and directing all cellular activities including protein synthesis.

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Classification. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Classification concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of biology.
Core Theory

This section of Classification builds on foundational knowledge. In biology, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 4.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Classification. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 4.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Classification. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.
Common examination questions on Classification include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Classification. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: The cell
questions.explanation: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for energy production in cells?
questions.answer: Mitochondria
questions.explanation: Mitochondria carry out aerobic respiration to produce ATP — the cell's energy currency. Hence called the 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.question: What is photosynthesis?
options: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
The breakdown of glucose for energy
The absorption of minerals from soil
questions.answer: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light energy → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants convert light energy to chemical energy stored in glucose.

questions.question: What is osmosis?
options: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.answer: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the passive movement of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane from a region of higher water potential to lower.

questions.question: What molecule carries genetic information?
questions.answer: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)
questions.explanation: DNA carries the genetic code in sequences of bases (A, T, G, C) that determine the structure of proteins.

questions.question: What is the function of chloroplasts?
questions.answer: Site of photosynthesis
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis, capturing light energy to make glucose.

questions.question: In genetics, what is a dominant allele?
options: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
One that is only expressed when two copies are present
questions.answer: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
questions.explanation: A dominant allele is expressed in the phenotype regardless of whether it is heterozygous or homozygous.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
options: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.answer: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the passive movement of particles down a concentration gradient — no energy (ATP) is required.

questions.question: Which blood component carries oxygen?
questions.answer: Red blood cells (haemoglobin)
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain haemoglobin, which binds to oxygen in the lungs and releases it in body tissues.

questions.question: What is the role of the nucleus?
options: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.answer: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the control centre of the cell, containing DNA and directing all cellular activities including protein synthesis.

questions.question: What is respiration?
options: The process of releasing energy from glucose in cells
questions.answer: The process of releasing energy from glucose in cells
questions.explanation: Cellular respiration: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ATP. It releases energy from glucose for cell use.

questions.question: What is natural selection?
options: Organisms with favourable traits survive and reproduce more
All organisms evolving at the same rate
questions.answer: Organisms with favourable traits survive and reproduce more
questions.explanation: Natural selection is Darwin's mechanism: individuals with traits better suited to the environment survive and pass on those traits.

questions.question: What organ filters blood in humans?
questions.answer: Kidneys
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter blood to remove waste products (urea, excess water, salts), producing urine.

questions.question: What is the function of stomata in leaves?
questions.answer: Gas exchange and transpiration
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores in leaves that allow CO₂ in and O₂ out during photosynthesis, and water vapour out during transpiration.

questions.question: Which part of the neurone carries impulses away from the cell body?
questions.answer: Axon
questions.explanation: The axon is the long fibre that carries nerve impulses away from the cell body to other neurons or effectors.

questions.question: What is the function of enzymes?
options: Biological catalysts that speed up reactions
questions.answer: Biological catalysts that speed up reactions
questions.explanation: Enzymes are protein catalysts that lower activation energy and speed up biochemical reactions without being consumed.

questions.question: What gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.answer: Carbon dioxide (CO₂)
questions.explanation: Plants absorb CO₂ from the air through stomata during photosynthesis, converting it to glucose using light energy.

questions.question: What is a food chain?
options: A sequence showing how energy passes from one organism to another
A list of foods animals eat
questions.answer: A sequence showing how energy passes from one organism to another
questions.explanation: A food chain shows the transfer of energy from producers (plants) through consumers. Example: Grass → Rabbit → Fox.

questions.question: What is the function of the cell membrane?
options: Controls what enters and leaves the cell (selectively permeable)
questions.answer: Controls what enters and leaves the cell (selectively permeable)
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is selectively permeable, controlling the passage of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.question: What is mitosis?
options: Cell division producing two genetically identical daughter cells
questions.answer: Cell division producing two genetically identical daughter cells
questions.explanation: Mitosis is cell division for growth and repair, producing two daughter cells with identical chromosomes to the parent cell (diploid).

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Classification concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of biology.
Core Theory

This section of Classification builds on foundational knowledge. In biology, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 6.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Classification. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 6.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Classification. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.
Common examination questions on Classification include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Classification. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: The cell
questions.explanation: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for energy production in cells?
questions.answer: Mitochondria
questions.explanation: Mitochondria carry out aerobic respiration to produce ATP — the cell's energy currency. Hence called the 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.question: What is photosynthesis?
options: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
The breakdown of glucose for energy
The absorption of minerals from soil
questions.answer: The process by which plants make food using sunlight
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light energy → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants convert light energy to chemical energy stored in glucose.

questions.question: What is osmosis?
options: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.answer: Movement of water from high to low water potential through a semi-permeable membrane
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the passive movement of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane from a region of higher water potential to lower.

questions.question: What molecule carries genetic information?
questions.answer: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)
questions.explanation: DNA carries the genetic code in sequences of bases (A, T, G, C) that determine the structure of proteins.

questions.question: What is the function of chloroplasts?
questions.answer: Site of photosynthesis
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis, capturing light energy to make glucose.

questions.question: In genetics, what is a dominant allele?
options: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
One that is only expressed when two copies are present
questions.answer: One that is expressed even when only one copy is present
questions.explanation: A dominant allele is expressed in the phenotype regardless of whether it is heterozygous or homozygous.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
options: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.answer: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the passive movement of particles down a concentration gradient — no energy (ATP) is required.

questions.question: Which blood component carries oxygen?
questions.answer: Red blood cells (haemoglobin)
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain haemoglobin, which binds to oxygen in the lungs and releases it in body tissues.

questions.question: What is the role of the nucleus?
options: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.answer: Controls cell activities and contains DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the control centre of the cell, containing DNA and directing all cellular activities including protein synthesis.

questions.question: What is respiration?
options: The process of releasing energy from glucose in cells
questions.answer: The process of releasing energy from glucose in cells
questions.explanation: Cellular respiration: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ATP. It releases energy from glucose for cell use.

questions.question: What is natural selection?
options: Organisms with favourable traits survive and reproduce more
All organisms evolving at the same rate
questions.answer: Organisms with favourable traits survive and reproduce more
questions.explanation: Natural selection is Darwin's mechanism: individuals with traits better suited to the environment survive and pass on those traits.

questions.question: What organ filters blood in humans?
questions.answer: Kidneys
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter blood to remove waste products (urea, excess water, salts), producing urine.

questions.question: What is the function of stomata in leaves?
questions.answer: Gas exchange and transpiration
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores in leaves that allow CO₂ in and O₂ out during photosynthesis, and water vapour out during transpiration.

questions.question: Which part of the neurone carries impulses away from the cell body?
questions.answer: Axon
questions.explanation: The axon is the long fibre that carries nerve impulses away from the cell body to other neurons or effectors.

questions.question: What is the function of enzymes?
options: Biological catalysts that speed up reactions
questions.answer: Biological catalysts that speed up reactions
questions.explanation: Enzymes are protein catalysts that lower activation energy and speed up biochemical reactions without being consumed.

questions.question: What gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.answer: Carbon dioxide (CO₂)
questions.explanation: Plants absorb CO₂ from the air through stomata during photosynthesis, converting it to glucose using light energy.

questions.question: What is a food chain?
options: A sequence showing how energy passes from one organism to another
A list of foods animals eat
questions.answer: A sequence showing how energy passes from one organism to another
questions.explanation: A food chain shows the transfer of energy from producers (plants) through consumers. Example: Grass → Rabbit → Fox.

questions.question: What is the function of the cell membrane?
options: Controls what enters and leaves the cell (selectively permeable)
questions.answer: Controls what enters and leaves the cell (selectively permeable)
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is selectively permeable, controlling the passage of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.question: What is mitosis?
options: Cell division producing two genetically identical daughter cells
questions.answer: Cell division producing two genetically identical daughter cells
questions.explanation: Mitosis is cell division for growth and repair, producing two daughter cells with identical chromosomes to the parent cell (diploid).
